file.content:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size           — report .text size for each assembly function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function

TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm

# Assembly sources
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Functions to test (must have test_<name>.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS = sinf cosf tanf atanf fabsf ceilf floorf expf sinhf coshf tanhf \
        logf log10f sqrtf asinf acosf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf modff \
        fpclassifyf

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see compile commands)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),0)
  Q = @
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q =
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
endif

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

# For each function, build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size: $(ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt

.PHONY: all size test-all clean $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))

content.text:
The main targets are:

- **`make all`** — build all opt and ref ELFs (default)
- **`make test-all`** — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs for all functions
- **`make test-<func>`** — test a single function (e.g., `make test-sinf`, `make test-cosf`)
- **`make size`** — report `.text` size for each assembly function
- **`make clean`** — remove all build artifacts

Available per-function `test-*` targets: `test-sinf`, `test-cosf`, `test-tanf`, `test-atanf`, `test-fabsf`, `test-ceilf`, `test-floorf`, `test-expf`, `test-sinhf`, `test-coshf`, `test-tanhf`, `test-logf`, `test-log10f`, `test-sqrtf`, `test-asinf`, `test-acosf`, `test-fmodf`, `test-powf`, `test-frexpf`, `test-ldexpf`, `test-modff`, `test-fpclassifyf`.

message.content: how to list all test-* functions in the cmd line

content.text:
```sh
make -pn | grep -oP '^test-\S+(?=:)' | sort
```

Or more simply, since `FUNCS` is defined in the Makefile:

```sh
make -p -n | grep '^FUNCS' 
```

Then each function `foo` in `FUNCS` has a `test-foo` target.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size           — report .text size for each assembly function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function

TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm

# Assembly sources
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Functions to test (must have test_<name>.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS = sinf cosf tanf atanf fabsf ceilf floorf expf sinhf coshf tanhf \
        logf log10f sqrtf asinf acosf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf modff \
        fpclassifyf

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see compile commands)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),0)
  Q = @
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q =
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
endif

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

# For each function, build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size: $(ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt

.PHONY: all size test-all clean $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))

lines: +.PHONY: all size test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))

content.text: Done. Now `make list` shows all top-level targets and per-function test targets.
